title: Teen Expectant/New Parent Assistance - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Youth Services > Teen Expectant/New Parent Assistance 68 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter At Risk Youth 211INFO 211INFO MATERNAL AND CHILD HEALTH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF 211info's Maternal and Child Health Program provides specialized information, referral, and follow-up services to individuals and family members of all genders covering needs and questions related to pregnancy, maternal and child health, sexual and reproductive health, and women's health across the life span, including resources for women with breast cancer. Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email 211 Visit Website More Details UMATILLA-MORROW COMMUNITY HEAD START HEALTHY FAMILIES OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a home visiting program that focuses on strengthening the parent-child relationship to assure healthy child growth and development. Home visitors support parents in cultivating and strengthening a nurturing, positive relationship with their baby at each visit. Parents receive information on topics like child development, infant care and keeping their baby healthy, and learn what is going on in their communities to support new families. A trained home visitor coaches families as they build their skills as parents and help their baby be safe, healthy and learn. 110 NE 4th Street Hermiston, OR 97838 Eligibility: Expectant families must enroll during pregnancy or before baby is 90 days old Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 667-6091 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ASHER COMMUNITY HEALTH CENTER BABIES FIRST! Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A public health nurse (PHN) home visiting program that partners with families to improve pregnancy, family and child health outcomes. Provides health and developmental screenings, health and nutrition education, infant and child development tips, breastfeeding support, and referrals to community resources that center on the family's needs. 712 Jay Street Fossil, OR 97830 Eligibility: Perinatal people, infants and children 0-5, and parent or primary caregivers with a higher risk for health issues. Risk factors may include prematurity, teenage parents, at-risk caregiver, domestic violence, drug-exposed infants. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm, Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 763-2725 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BAKER COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH BABIES FIRST! Nurses also help connect families with community resources to promote parents’ health and children’s well-being. 530 NW 27th Street Corvallis, OR 97330 Eligibility: Birth, adoptive, or resource families with a newborn 0-12 weeks Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 760-1834 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CENTER FOR HUMAN DEVELOPMENT BABIES FIRST! Home visitors support parents in cultivating and strengthening a nurturing, positive relationship with their baby at each visit. Parents receive information on topics like child development, infant care and keeping their baby healthy, and learning what is going on in their communities to support new families. A trained family support specialist engages with parents to nurture their baby in a safe and healthy environment that encourages learning. 16518 SE River Road Milwaukie, OR 97267 Eligibility: Families must score positive on the New Baby Questionnare needs assessment and then be enrolled during the prenatal through infant stages (90 days or younger) however once enrolled, children can remain enrolled until 36 months of age. Hours: Monday-Thursday 8:30am-5:00pm. Evening hours are available for home visits as needed. Email (503) 675-4565 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CLACKAMAS COUNTY PUBLIC HEALTH BABIES FIRST!
